original_abstract: The crystal structure of Cd3As2, a high-mobility semimetal known for hosting electronic Dirac particles, has been reinvestigated using single-crystal X-ray diffraction. Our analysis reveals that the material is centrosymmetric, contrary to previous reports of a noncentrosymmetric structure. Cd3As2 crystallizes in the tetragonal space group $I4_1/acd$ with unit cell parameters $a = 12.633(3)$ and $c = 25.427(7)$ Å, forming a distorted superstructure of the antifluorite ($M_2X$) type. This architecture consists of distorted $Cd_6\square_2$ cubes (where $\square$ represents a vacancy) arranged in parallel columns that stack with opposing chirality. Electronic structure calculations based on this centrosymmetric model demonstrate that the presence of inversion symmetry prevents spin splitting; consequently, all bands remain spin-degenerate. This results in a four-fold degenerate bulk Dirac point at the Fermi energy along the $\Gamma-Z$ direction in the Brillouin zone, establishing Cd3As2 as a three-dimensional electronic analogue of graphene. Furthermore, scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) identifies a $2 \times 2$ surface reconstruction on the $(112)$ cleavage plane, while morphological analysis confirms that needle-like crystals grow along the $[110]$ axis.
humanized_abstract: The crystal structure of the high mobility semimetal Cd3As2, which hosts electronic Dirac particles, has been re-examined by means of single crystal X-ray diffraction experiments, and found to be centrosymmetric in contrast with previous reports of a noncentrosymmetric structure, thus confirming the Pmn21 space group assignment (ref. [17]) with some small deviations and being in full agreement with the one recently reported by ref. [17]. In this centrosymmetric phase, Cd3As2 crystallizes in the tetragonal I4_1/acd space group with a = 12.633(3) Å and c = 25.427(7) Å and an antifluorite (M2X) type distorted structure. Such structure is composed of undistorted Cd6square2 (whereasquare2 stands for a tetrahedral vacancy) octamers which are placed along the c axis in a column and the + and - chiral columns are alternatively stacked along c. First principles band structure calculations based on this centrosymmetric structure shows that the inversion symmetry of the structure implies that the spin splitting of the bands does not occur and that the bands remain spin degenerate. As a result, a four-fold degenerate bulk Dirac point, which is similar to that of graphene, is found at the Fermi energy along the Γ-Z line in the Brillouin zone. Scanning tunneling microscopy (STM) experiments, which were carried out on the (112) surface of a Cd3As2 single crystalline sample, have evidenced a 2 × 2 reconstruction on this surface and the morphology of a needle-like crystal growing along the [110] direction has been determined.

In this centrosymmetric phase, Cd3As2 crystallizes in the tetragonal I4_1/acd space group with a = 12.633(3) Å and c = 25.427(7) Å and an antifluorite (M2X) type distorted structure. Such structure is composed of undistorted Cd6square2 (whereasquare2 stands for a tetrahedral vacancy) octamers which are placed along the c axis in a column and the + and - chiral columns are alternatively stacked along c. First principles band structure calculations based on this centrosymmetric structure shows that the inversion symmetry of the structure implies that the spin splitting of the bands does not occur and that the bands remain spin degenerate.
